Black Ice Boots  
 

Lore Item
Slot: FEET
AC: 21
INT: +5
SV FIRE: +10 SV COLD: +10
WT: 5.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: WAR SHD ROG BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Black Ice Boots
Lore Item
Slot: FEET
AC: 21
INT: +5
SV FIRE: +10 SV COLD: +10
WT: 5.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: WAR SHD ROG BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Velious Chain 2
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):40, 40, 40
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:0 pp 1 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:844
Lucy Entry By:Mordrakken
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 08:32:51
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Average Price: 1,250pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 50

NPC Name
Errkak Icepaw
The Brood Mother

Uploaded November 27th, 2008

Post Comment
Do indeed drop still.
QuoteReply
# Nov 04 2007 at 1:41 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1655684328652
View User Forum
6 posts
These do drop still. Got some on a Brood Mother. Fairly rare.
Not as rare as the BP. ^.^;
▲ Page top
Do these still drop?
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2002 at 2:09 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759552183
33 posts
I have looked for these endlessly for weeks and it seems no one has ever seen or heard of them. Do they still drop? Are they fake? Anyone have any recent info about them at all? I'm thinkin my SK would love a pair but I'd hate to keep wasting my time if they aren't available anymore. Any information at all would be appreciated...
▲ Page top
RE: Do these still drop?
QuoteReply
# Sep 11 2002 at 10:05 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592254202386
37 posts
Not fake, I have a pair that I swap out with barbed dragonscale boots when I need resists on my 50 sk.

Black Ice Looks...
QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2001 at 12:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248326367
6 posts
Like black Ry'Gorr. My rogue's got a pair of the greaves and the sleeves, and they're the Ry'Gorr texture, just black. It's very nifty looking. ;)

Syn
▲ Page top
Ry'gorr boots...
QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2001 at 8:38 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250031890
Scholar
35 posts
Heh, you can't really say that these look like Ry'gorr, because there aren't any ry'gorr boots...

Seriously, tho...ARE there any boots out there that'll match ry'gorr pants? I'm not too concerned about how I look, but in almost every visable slot ry'gorr was the best, cheapest/easiest choice for me (plus It's a nice camp)...Except for the feet.
▲ Page top
RE: Ry'gorr boots...
QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2001 at 12:47 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757638677
38 posts
I found that Cougar Skin boots (quest in Iceclad) look pretty nce with Rygor. Deep black boots with a gold trim around the top on a HEF Ranger. Just look under Iceclad Quests
▲ Page top
RE: Ry'gorr boots...
QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2001 at 9:05 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245673499
47 posts
True, there are no Ry'gorr boots; however, Sentry armor has the same graphics as Ry'gorr and there ARE Sentry Boots. My ranger has a set of this armor and he looks quite spiffy in it :).
▲ Page top
How is it...
QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2001 at 12:19 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592756803348
9 posts
.. that these only have 5ac less than what the sleaves and legs do combined???
▲ Page top
RE: How is it...
QuoteReply
# Sep 08 2001 at 7:50 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758653941
14 posts
much harder to get, fewer classes can wear so the higher ac is justifiable i guess
▲ Page top
Odd...
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2001 at 6:28 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246547941
16 posts
Seems odd thta bards cant use these. Most items that are WAR, SK, and ROG usually have bards included, why not these?
▲ Page top
RE: Odd...
QuoteReply
# Sep 23 2002 at 6:03 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759294285
126 posts
The AC and stats on these are significantly better than the stats on the other Black Ice items. 21 AC is the same AC as the Thurgadin Shadow Knight quest boots, for instance. 13 AC is far less than the Thurgadin Shadow Knight quest arms :)
▲ Page top
RE: Odd...
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2001 at 9:23 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965846855
151 posts
Actually, the classes on it are *very* odd, since there's no consistency about them. Rogues are chain wearers, Warriors and Shadowknights are plate wearers. The other plate wearers (bard, cleric, paladin) and chain wearers (ranger, shaman) aren't on the list. The only thing I can think of for the class restrictions is that the boots are "evil," and only usable by classes that are distinctively not "good." Though why warriors would be included and shamans not, in that case, I don't know.

Very weird, anyway.
▲ Page top
RE: Odd...
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2001 at 11:34 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592247222988
5 posts
Actually, it sort of makes sense from a role playing view. "Black Ice" does not sound like something that generally goodly-inclined cleric and paladins would want to have something to do with, rangers also. However, Rogues(Who're rather on the shady side), SKs(who are VERY on the shady side) and warriors, who have no trouble being on the shady side, would lean towards "black ice" from this standpoint
Just my after work rambling...
Reodd Jadai
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 11 2001 at 8:49 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) hi dark elf clerix sanks infroz
RE: Odd...
QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2001 at 2:39 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763719754
16 posts
My only dissent is that Black Ice Sleeces are Pally/Ranger wearable.
▲ Page top
RE: Odd...
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2001 at 9:51 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1656893782646
Scholar
158 posts
Odder still is that the sleeves and leggings do have all those other classes on them...just the boots do not.
▲ Page top
RE: Odd...
QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2001 at 1:42 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763525330
Scholar
31 posts
The other classes could have been un-intentionaly left off the list. If the other pieces do include all of those other classes, then I would gather that the suit itself was probably meant for more classes then the three listed.

On the other side, I seem to remeber some other armor that had an odd piece like this too (a more restrictive piece) but for the life of me can not remember what it was. I guess it couldn't hurt to send /feedback and mention it (if you have these and one other piece), to see if someone just messed up on the allowable classes.

/shrug
▲ Page top
RE: Odd...
QuoteReply
# Aug 11 2001 at 2:15 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246460789
View User Forum
513 posts
I believe the armour you're thinking of was Gnomeskin, from the ToFS.
